ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general biscuit food product class; no specific sandwich biscuit with jam filling subclass exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_description: Biscuits and cookies — FoodEx2 composite category for sweet biscuits and filled biscuits
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A000N covers biscuits and cookies broadly; no dedicated filled sandwich biscuit code exists. Closest parent match applied.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve pack of sandwich biscuits with jam filling weighs approximately 30 g, comprising two biscuit pieces sandwiched with fruit jam, commonly sold in school canteens and convenience stores in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Sandwich Biscuit (Jam Filling) — Singapore HPB Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sandwich Biscuit (Jam Filling), cr_id F11080055. All core macros and secondary nutrients are sourced from this record at confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 70 assigned based on analogous sweet biscuit / filled biscuit category from the University of Sydney GI Database. Sweet biscuits and filled sandwich biscuits with refined flour and high sugar content are consistently reported in the GI range 65–75 (high). Glycemic load per 30 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (70 × 19.1) / 100 = 13.4. Confidence 0.65 applied given analogous rather than exact match.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar content (32.2 g/100 g) with low dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g); excess dietary sugar independently elevates blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this snack; despite moderate sodium (100 mg/100 g), the very high sugar load (32.2 g/100 g) independently raises cardiovascular and blood pressure risk and is not offset by the low fibre content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 32.2 g/100 g far exceeds the 20 g/100 g glycaemic ceiling; high GI (70) and high glycaemic load (13.4 per 30 g serving) with minimal fibre (2.0 g/100 g) will cause significant post-meal glucose spikes
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product entirely; the combination of very high sugar (32.2 g/100 g), high GI (70), and low dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g) will cause rapid and significant post-prandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (~13 g/100 g from palm oil), estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g from processed biscuit manufacturing), and very high sugar (32.2 g/100 g) collectively elevate LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid this snack; the high saturated fat content (~13 g/100 g), likely trans fat from industrial biscuit production, and very high sugar load all contribute to adverse lipid profiles and increased cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very high refined sugar (32.2 g/100 g) promotes pro-inflammatory mediators; palm oil-derived saturated fat (~13 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this snack; the combination of very high refined sugar, high saturated fat from palm oil, and likely trans fat from industrial processing promotes systemic inflammation and may worsen joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sandwich Biscuit with Jam Filling is a commercially manufactured ultra-processed snack consisting of two thin, crisp wheat-flour biscuits sandwiched together with a sweet fruit jam filling. The biscuits are baked and typically made with refined wheat flour, vegetable fat (commonly palm oil), sugar, salt, and raising agents, while the jam filling is a high-sugar fruit preserve. The texture is crisp and slightly crumbly on the outside with a soft, sticky, sweet jam centre. The flavour profile is predominantly sweet with a mild buttery or shortbread-like biscuit note. This product is widely sold in Singapore school canteens, convenience stores, and supermarkets as an affordable snack for children and adults. From a nutritional standpoint, this is a high-energy (521 kcal/100 g), high-fat (27.2 g/100 g), high-sugar (32.2 g/100 g), and high-carbohydrate (63.7 g/100 g) food with low dietary fibre (2.0 g/100 g) and moderate sodium (100 mg/100 g). The saturated fat content is estimated at approximately 13 g/100 g due to palm oil use. The glycaemic index is high (GI ~70) and the glycaemic load per 30 g serving is approximately 13.4, making this a poor choice for individuals managing blood glucose, blood pressure, or cardiovascular risk. It is not suitable for people with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Calcium and iron are reported as 0 mg/100 g in the HPB source. Monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, potassium, cholesterol, and micronutrient data are not available in the HPB source for this entry.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F11080055) at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at ~13 g/100 g based on palm oil composition typical for this product class; trans fat estimated at 0.3 g/100 g based on industrial biscuit manufacturing norms. GI assigned as analogous estimate (high, ~70) from University of Sydney GI Database for sweet filled biscuits. Overall confidence is high for macros and secondary nutrients from HPB; medium for saturated fat, trans fat, and GI estimates.
notes: 1. Calcium_mg is reported as 0.0 and iron_mg is reported as 0.0 in the HPB source (cr_id F11080055); these are confirmed zero values per the approved HPB Singapore Foodo database and are not estimated. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this sandwich biscuit (jam filling) entry are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database (F11080055) as of 2026. 3. Saturated fat (~13 g/100 g) is estimated based on palm oil fat composition typical for this biscuit category; the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 4. Trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) is an estimate based on industrial biscuit manufacturing norms; the HPB source does not report trans fat for this entry. 5. GI of 70 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweet filled biscuits; no exact GI measurement for this specific product was found. 6. This food is classified as ultra-processed (NOVA Group 4). 7. This food is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to very high sugar, high saturated fat, estimated trans fat, and high GI. 8. The food is Western/international in origin and sold widely in Singapore; no mandatory Singaporean-dish multilingual naming requirement applies as this is not a Singaporean dish by cuisine classification.
